message:
probe: confirm transformers.js has no cpu path for this model

tjs-cpu-probe.spec.ts iterates over (device, dtype) for
openai/privacy-filter using transformers.js 4.2.0 from the CDN, the
same version package.json pins. Every CPU-device attempt fails at
session creation:

  device=wasm dtype=q4f16  — "Failed to find kernel for com.microsoft.GatherBlockQuantize"
  device=wasm dtype=q4     — same
  device=wasm dtype=q8     — same
  device=wasm dtype=fp32   — same
  device=cpu  dtype=q4f16  — "Unsupported device: cpu"

ORT-Web's WASM backend has no GatherBlockQuantized implementation
(contrib op, WebGPU-only in the published bundle), so there is no
working tjs CPU path for this model. Our WasmBackend is, in practice,
the only ONNX-compatible CPU path for openai/privacy-filter.

Documented in CLAUDE.md already; this spec pins the evidence so future
Claude sessions don't re-derive it by trial and error.
